summary:
常見問題 (Q&A)  
Q1:  Futera Dots 膠原槍 為非醫材？  
A1:  Futera  Dots  possesses KFDA (Korea) and TFDA (Taiwan) medical device certification. It is a certified medical device.  
A2:  The treatment uses bipolar fractional radiofrequency (RF) technology. The probe contains 604 pin heating points (note: text says 64 pin in other sections, likely a typo in Q&A section) heating points. Each shot creates a microscopic wound in the collagen matrix, stimulating collagen regeneration.  
A8:  The treatment is suitable for mild to moderate aging, fine lines, large pores, dull complexion, and acne scars. It is particularly recommended for Asian skin types due to lower risk of post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation (PIH).  
A3:  Pre-treatment: Stop A-acid/retinoids, antibiotics, and steroids 2 months prior. Avoid NSAIDs 1 week prior.  
A4: Post-treatment: Avoid sun exposure, do not pick scabs, use sterile saline for cleaning, avoid harsh skincare, sauna, swimming, sweating, alcohol, and smoking for one week.  
A4: The treatment is suitable for those with mild to moderate aging, large pores, dull skin, fine lines, and acne scars. It positioned as a "gateway" treatment for medical aesthetics newcomers and those on a T

Based on the provided document for the **Futera Dots 膠原槍 (Futera Dots Collagen Gun)**, here is an exhaustive professional summary focusing on clinical mechanisms, procedural protocols, risk management, and patient care.

### **1. Product Overview & Clinical Mechanism**
*   **Product Name:** Futera Dots (Brand: "Aitraxi" / 艾特拉喜)
*   **Manufacturer:** Asterasys (South Korea)
*   **Regulatory Status:** Certified Medical Device (TFDA in Taiwan, KFDA in Korea).
*   **Core Technology:** **Bipolar Fractional Radiofrequency (RF)**.
    *   Unlike traditional monopolar RF (deep, volumetric heating) or standard fractional lasers, Futera Dots uses a **fractional approach**.
    *   **Probe Specification:** 64-pin heating points arranged in a 1.1 x 1.1 cm² square matrix.
